calibration.method: planar target with stripe at LiDAR scan height
calibration.stripe_annotation: manual centerline samples in synchronized frames

notes: The camera is measured 3.0 inches behind and 4.75 inches above the LiDAR housing center.
The 0.2-inch lateral offset is provisionally camera-right, hence negative left.
The camera is locked to the calibrated 1920x1080 10-bit sensor mode.
The camera was physically tilted downward and secured before the rotational fit.
The effective vertical translation is 5.32 inches from the modeled scan-plane reference to the camera optical center.
The effective vertical translation is preserved separately from the 4.75-inch housing-center measurement.
Angle offset, vertical translation, pitch, and roll were jointly fit from four complete scans at both 24 and 48 inches.
Do not move the camera or LiDAR mount without repeating extrinsic calibration.
A 36-inch held-out target passed without retuning after the 24- and 48-inch fit.
